questions.question: What is climate?
questions.correct_answer: The average weather conditions of a place observed over a long period of time
questions.explanation: Climate describes long-term atmospheric conditions such as temperature, rainfall and wind patterns.

questions.question: What is weather?
questions.correct_answer: The condition of the atmosphere at a particular place and time
questions.explanation: Weather changes frequently and may vary from hour to hour or day to day.

questions.question: What are the main elements of weather and climate?
questions.correct_answer: Temperature, atmospheric pressure, wind, humidity and precipitation
questions.explanation: These elements together determine the atmospheric conditions of a place.

questions.question: Why is the climate of India called monsoon type?
questions.correct_answer: Because it is influenced by seasonal reversal of winds called monsoons
questions.explanation: The monsoon winds bring most of the rainfall to India each year.

questions.question: What are climatic controls?
questions.correct_answer: Factors that influence the climate of a place
questions.explanation: These factors determine temperature, rainfall and wind conditions in different regions.

questions.question: Name any two climatic controls.
questions.explanation: Latitude determines the amount of solar energy received and altitude influences temperature.

questions.question: What are the southwest monsoon winds?
questions.correct_answer: Moist winds that bring rainfall to India during the monsoon season
questions.explanation: These winds originate over warm oceans and move towards the Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: Which season in India is associated with 'loo' winds?
questions.explanation: Loo are hot and dry winds that blow in northern India during summer months.

questions.question: When does the cold weather season occur in India?
questions.explanation: During this season temperatures fall, especially in northern India.

questions.question: Which place receives the highest rainfall in the world?
questions.explanation: It receives extremely heavy rainfall due to the influence of monsoon winds and relief features.

notes.title: Monsoon Climate of India
notes.content: India has a monsoon type of climate characterized by seasonal reversal of winds that bring rainfall during the monsoon season.

notes.title: Climatic Controls
notes.content: Latitude, altitude, pressure and wind systems, distance from the sea, ocean currents and relief features influence the climate of a region.

notes.title: Seasons of India
notes.content: India experiences four main seasons: cold weather season, hot weather season, advancing monsoon season and retreating monsoon season.

notes.title: Distribution of Rainfall
notes.content: Rainfall varies greatly across India. Northeastern regions receive very heavy rainfall while western Rajasthan and Ladakh receive very little.

notes.title: Importance of Monsoon
notes.content: The monsoon influences agriculture, water supply and the livelihood of millions of people in India.

facts.fact: The word monsoon comes from the Arabic word 'mausim', meaning season.
Southwest monsoon winds bring most of the rainfall to India.
Rajasthan and Ladakh receive very low rainfall compared to other regions of India.
The monsoon acts as a unifying bond because it influences agriculture and the economy across India.
